issue_title: Session cross-reference / transaction mismatch
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

#20793 introduced a memory leak similar to #19396.

Context contains objects from session `S1`.
Session `S1` contains objects that refer to transaction `T1`, and often to session `S1` (see e.g. [`RealmAdapter`](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/jpa/RealmAdapter.java#L53)).

Now in the new variant of `runJobInTransactionWithResult`, a new session `S2` is created with transaction `T2`.
It however refers objects from `S1`, and until that transaction is completed, it prevents its garbage collection.

Furthermore, the objects in the new session `S2` context are controlled by transaction `T1`, while they should be controlled by transaction `T2`.

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

Objects from a transaction of a session are controlled by that transaction.

### Actual behavior

Objects from the context of a session are controlled by parent transaction.

### How to Reproduce?

Leverage new variant of `runJobInTransactionWithResult`.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Session cross-reference / transaction mismatch
Closes https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/20855

issue_title: Control redirect after password reset
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

account/ui

### Describe the bug

After a user has reset their password, they are sent to a "random" redirect.
This happens if the user is sent directly to the reset-credentials endpoint (skipping the KC login page).
The "random" redirect is the first "Valid redirect URI", which are sorted by their hash value. Hence the quoted random.

### Version

18 (RH-SSO 7.6)

### Expected behavior

I should be able to control the redirect somewhat.

**Here are several suggested solutions**
1. Redirect to "Base URL". Base is described in the documentation as "Default URL to use when the auth server needs to redirect or link back to the client". This sounds as it should be the fallback if no redirect is found.
2. Being able to change sort order of "Valid Redirect URIs"
3. Sort "Valid Redirect URIs" alphabetically.
4. Support "redirect_uri" query-param for reset-credentials endpoint. Also requested in #15279 

### Actual behavior

The user is redirected to the first "valid redirect uri", which can't be controlled.

When "Valid Redirect URIs" are loaded they are put into a HashSet
See org.keycloak.models.utils.RepresentationToModel line 571
`resource.setRedirectUris(new HashSet<>(redirectUris));`

When loading the redirectUri, the first (with the lowest hash value) is set.
See org.keycloak.services.resources.LoginActionsService line 422
`redirectUri = RedirectUtils.getFirstValidRedirectUri(session, client.getRootUrl(), client.getRedirectUris());`

### How to Reproduce?

1. have a client with multiple "Valid Redirect URIs" and Base URL set
2. Go to {KEYCLOAK}/auth/realms/{REALM}/login-actions/reset-credentials?client_id={CLIENT_ID}
3. Enter email and receive a “reset password”-email with a link
4. Click link
5. Enter new password twice and submit
6. Get redirect to first "Valid Redirect URIs"

### Anything else?

The "Valid Redirect URIs" should only be used for validation and never for fallback.
They can contains wildcards (*) and therefore can be invalid URLs.
"Base URL" is a much better candidate for this.
commit_message:
Remove random redirect after password reset (#27076)

closes #20867

Signed-off-by: mposolda <mposolda@gmail.com>


Co-authored-by: Ricardo Martin <rmartinc@redhat.com>

issue_title: Can't use database schema with a dash in its name (liquibase, postgres)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

I created a clean lab environment with keycloak v21.1.1 and postgres 12, both via docker container. The database was prepared with dbname `sso-stack-lab`, schema `keycloak-lab` and a user `keycloak-lab`. Configfile `keycloak.conf` is mounted from local host into the container, setting `db=postgres`, `db-url-host=localhost`, `db-username=keycloak-lab`, `db-password=mySecretPWD`, `db-schema=keycloak-lab`

When starting keycloak (from a custom image) I get the error `liquibase.exception.DatabaseException: ERROR: syntax error at or near "-"`

After stumbling across #17276 I changed the database name to no avail. Next I tried changing the schema name to `keycloaklab` (removing the dash) and now it works. I wasnt able to further pinpoint the problem, but after removing the dash from schema name, but with username name and database name still having a dash, it works.

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

Using a database schema name with a dash, keycloak starts and works as expected.

### Actual behavior

When starting keycloak (from a custom image with added certificates and build run done) with a database schema name including a dash I get the error `liquibase.exception.DatabaseException: ERROR: syntax error at or near "-"` and keycloak will fail to start.

### How to Reproduce?

Run a clean install of keycloak, using postgres with a database schema name including a dash.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Introduce a workaround for liquibase bug to allow use database schema with a dash in its name

Closes #20870

issue_title: Emails with non-ascii characters are not allowed since v21.0.0
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

user-profile

### Describe the bug

After upgrading from v20.0.0 to v21.1.1 we noticed that emails containing non-ascii characters are not accepted anymore.

This is preventing us from upgrading to the latest versions, which has solved a lot of security vulnerabilities

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

When creating or updating users keycloak versions 21 and above, Emails with non-ascii characters such as `diegø@gmail.com` should be allowed. 

### Actual behavior

When creating or updating users in keycloak versions 21 and above, Emails with non-ascii characters such as `diegø@gmail.com` result in invalid email error.

### How to Reproduce?

Start a container with keycloak with any version after v21.0.0 (i.e. v21.1.1)

`docker run -it --rm -p 8080:8080 -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN=admin -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin  quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:21.1.1 start-dev --features=scripts`

Going to the admin portal -> users -> add user -> fill in the email input field with an email address that contains non-ascii characters, such as `diegø@gmail.com`. You will get an "invalid email" error even though it is a valid email.

![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/14559432/13385a66-ea1d-4ce5-8d4f-4d8406ef1c54)

You can confirm that this used to work in version below v21.0.0 by repeating the same steps above but spinning up a container with any version below v21.0.0.

`docker run -it --rm -p 8080:8080 -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN=admin -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin  quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:20.0.0 start-dev --features=scripts`

![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/14559432/db347aa5-7081-49d0-9d5f-7c88f6140310)

### Anything else?

My very wild guess is that PR #15043 introduced this issue as it seems to be using the `[EmailValidator`](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/fbfdb547459dfa4f82256a60aef03f6b32dba1dd/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/validate/validators/EmailValidator.java#L43) which uses a regex that doesn't account for non-ascii characters.

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/857b02be6378f8a26c3c809ac72683257197691a#diff-45cc3f2945c63a4269281feeea8d34243f7179eb761dba4d8627c34da87c648c
commit_message:
Emails with non-ascii characters are not allowed since v21.0.0
closes #20878

issue_title: Key length is limited to 4000 characters
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

I am trying to add an RSA certificate which is 5517 characters long in it's PEM presentation, using either Terraform's keycloak_realm_keystore_rsa provider or GUI: Realm settings -> Keys -> Providers -> Add provider -> rsa -> Enter a 5517 character long PEM into field 'X509 Certificate'.

This fails with an unexpected error. Terraform says '500 Internal Server Error. Response body: {"error":"unknown_error"}'

### Version

20.0.5

### Expected behavior

Long rsa certificates should work, just like certificates shorter than 4000 characters in their PEM format

### Actual behavior

This fails with an unexpected error. Terraform says '500 Internal Server Error. Response body: {"error":"unknown_error"}'

Keycloak log says:
`Caused by: org.postgresql.util.PSQLException: ERROR: value too long for type character varying(4000)`

### How to Reproduce?

- Have an RSA certificate and its private key in PEM format. The certificate should be longer than 4000 characters
- In GUI: Go to your realm -> Realm settings -> Keys -> Providers -> Add provider -> rsa -> Enter the PEM into field 'X509 Certificate', also fill the other required fields
- Click 'Save'

### Anything else?

This is clearly a problem with the 4000 character limit on the database schema:

On psql, logged in as the keycloak database user:
```
keycloak=> \d component_config
                     Table "public.component_config"
    Column    |          Type           | Collation | Nullable | Default 
--------------+-------------------------+-----------+----------+---------
 id           | character varying(36)   |           | not null | 
 component_id | character varying(36)   |           | not null | 
 name         | character varying(255)  |           | not null | 
 value        | character varying(4000) |           |          | 
Indexes:
    "constr_component_config_pk" PRIMARY KEY, btree (id)
    "idx_compo_config_compo" btree (component_id)
Foreign-key constraints:
    "fk_component_config" FOREIGN KEY (component_id) REFERENCES component(id)
```
I tripled the character limit:
```
keycloak=> alter table component_config alter column value type character varying(12000);
ALTER TABLE

keycloak=> \d component_config
                     Table "public.component_config"
    Column    |           Type           | Collation | Nullable | Default 
--------------+--------------------------+-----------+----------+---------
 id           | character varying(36)    |           | not null | 
 component_id | character varying(36)    |           | not null | 
 name         | character varying(255)   |           | not null | 
 value        | character varying(12000) |           |          | 
Indexes:
    "constr_component_config_pk" PRIMARY KEY, btree (id)
    "idx_compo_config_compo" btree (component_id)
Foreign-key constraints:
    "fk_component_config" FOREIGN KEY (component_id) REFERENCES component(id)
```
... and the problem is gone. I can now add my rsa certificate.

commit_message:
Removes the check for full readiness of 3 replicas

In the ci environment the amount of time between 3 pods being created
and all being ready seems highly variable.  Also since we currently lack
the status information to determine if a deployment is not progressing,
it's best just to remove this check.  The latter check asserting full
readiness of 2 replicas will suffice.

Closes #20888

issue_title: Keycloak's default http client doesn't check HTTP response code
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When retrieving information via HTTP like for example information from a discovery endpoint like in #20802, Keycloak will not check the HTTP response code. 

This leads to a situation that data is processed when it shouldn't, as the caller is assuming it is a successful call. 
While `HttpClientProvider#get()` can return null, no caller ever checks that, leading to NPEs.

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

On non-2xx-responses, an error should be logged and the response shouldn't be processed any futher.

### Actual behavior

Non-2xx-responses are processed.

### How to Reproduce?

* Start Keycloak locally on port 8080 (Quarkus default in start-dev)
* Go to *Identity Providers* and create a new one
* Use `http://localhost:8080/realms/master/.well-known/missing` as a URL that returns 404 to retrieve the settings

### Anything else?

While this issue is about handling the response code, a follow-up issue should revisit the interface again to ensure that the response is processed with the right encoding/content type. At the moment the input streams are read using Keycloak's encoding, while they should be parsed using the encoding returned by the called server.

This follow-up issue should be tackled once this issue has been resolved.
commit_message:
Handle HTTP response codes when retrieving data from remote endpoints

Closes #20895

issue_title: DefaultHttpClientFactory should handle the encoding of the response
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

As a follow-up to #20897, the response should respect the returned content type and its charset when handling the response in `DefaultHttpClientFactory`. 

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

A response is decoded using the correct charset.

### Actual behavior

An `InputStream` is returned and the callers will decode it later using the JVM's default charset.

### How to Reproduce?

(by code review)

### Anything else?

In the different places where this is being called, it would be better to return a readily decoded String instead of using an InputStream with an unknown encoding.
commit_message:
Instead of an InputStream that doesn't know about its encoding, use a String

Closes #20916

issue_title: org.keycloak.userprofile.UserProfileProvider is bounced on every realm change
issue_body:
Originally reported in [KEYCLOAK-19044](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-19044):

Whenever the realm is changed:

- the org.keycloak.userprofile.UserProfileProvider is added to the export/components if it was previously missing
- the org.keycloak.userprofile.UserProfileProvider is removed from the export/components if it was previously present
So the org.keycloak.userprofile.UserProfileProvider's presence is constantly bounced when the realm is changed.

Steps to reproduce:

- Start the docker image with: docker run -p 8080:8080 -e KEYCLOAK_USER=keycloak -e KEYCLOAK_PASSWORD=keycloak -e JAVA_OPTS="-Dkeycloak.profile.feature.scripts=enabled -Dkeycloak.profile.feature.upload_scripts=enabled" quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:15.0.1
- In the browser go to the localhost:8080
- Log in with keycloak/keycloak
- Create a new realm - ex 'jose'
- export the realm - the org.keycloak.userprofile.UserProfileProvider is not present
- Change the realm (ex set description to 'a')
- export the realm - the org.keycloak.userprofile.UserProfileProvider is present
- Change the ream (ex set description to 'b')
- export the realm - the org.keycloak.userprofile.UserProfileProvider is not present
- Change the realm (ex set the descriptio to 'c')
- export the realm - the org.keycloak.userprofile.UserProfileProvider is present
- ...

I was able to reproduce the issue with current main.

issue_title: Avoid loading classes and resources from new store if legacy is enabled
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

When starting the server using the legacy store you'll see the following message indicating that components specific to the JPA store are loaded into the resulting Hibernate Session Factory:

```
WARN  [org.keycloak.models.map.storage.jpa.hibernate.contributor.JpaMapFunctionContributor] (JPA Startup Thread) Dialect org.hibernate.dialect.H2Dialect, version: 2.1.210 not recognized.
```

There are similar places where code from the new store is changing the runtime (when using legacy/default storage layer). And some of them are only executing code for a specific storage layer, see https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/model/map-jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/map/storage/jpa/EventListenerIntegrator.java#L45.

A similar situation happens when loading service providers from `model/map-hot-rod/target/classes/META-INF/services/org.infinispan.protostream.SerializationContextInitializer`. The implementations from there are going to be registered even though the legacy store is in use.

Ideally, we should have the legacy and new stores isolated from each other at runtime.

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

Do not load or run initialization code from new store modules if using the legacy store.

### Actual behavior

See description

### How to Reproduce?

Run the server using `start-dev`.

### Anything else?

_No response_

Ignoring artifacts when running re-aug to isolate the current and new stores

Closes #20974

```
        at java.base@11.0.18/jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:43)
        at java.base@11.0.18/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:566)
        at org.jboss.resteasy.core.MethodInjectorImpl.invoke(MethodInjectorImpl.java:170)
        at org.jboss.resteasy.core.MethodInjectorImpl.invoke(MethodInjectorImpl.java:130)
        at org.jboss.resteasy.core.ResourceMethodInvoker.internalInvokeOnTarget(ResourceMethodInvoker.java:660)
        at org.jboss.resteasy.core.ResourceMethodInvoker.invokeOnTargetAfterFilter(ResourceMethodInvoker.java:524)
        at org.jboss.resteasy.core.ResourceMethodInvoker.lambda$invokeOnTarget$2(ResourceMethodInvoker.java:474
```

According to my investigation, the request process spends a lot of time in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/21.1.1/rest/admin-ui-ext/src/main/java/org/keycloak/admin/ui/rest/model/AuthenticationMapper.java#L35 . The `MAX_USED_BY==9` limit is useless, because none of our clients overrides the authentication flow.

Keycloak iterates on the full list of clients. Consequently, the full list of clients loads up in Hibernate 1st level cache leading to big performance degradation of the Hibernate autoflush process.

issue_title: Make sure identity providers are not send in realm GET requests and PUT requests used in "Realm settings"
issue_body:
### Description

The issue is that when some generic realm page is displayed in the admin console (for instance page http://localhost:8081/auth/admin/master/console/#/test/realm-settings with the basic settings for the `test` realm), then the admin console uses realm GET endpoint under the covers to download the data about realm. For instance it will send GET request to endpoint http://localhost:8081/auth/admin/realms/test, which will return JSON with the `test` realm. The issue is, that realm JSON contains the field `identityProviders` with all the realm identity providers. This doesn't scale in case that realm has many identity providers.

This issue applies for many pages through the admin console (pretty much all the subtabs under left admin console tab `Realm Settings`). However most of the pages don't need identity providers at all.

Another issue with this is, that when some realm settings page is updated by admin console, the subsequent realm PUT REST call includes the realm JSON including identityProviders (pretty much same JSON which was returned by GET endpoint). This caused the error when realm has many identity providers due the event was too long and particular DB column was overflowed, but it is possible that there are multiple issues (especially when count of identity providers would be very big).

### Possible solution

We can handle this by creating "admin console specific" REST endpoint where we can trim identityProviders as well as other possibly redundant stuff. This can be done under https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/tree/main/rest/admin-ui-ext . Admin console can consume this on most of the places (subtabs of "Realm Settings" and other pages not directly dealing with "Identity providers").

One alternative approach is to directly update realm GET endpoint to not return `identityProviders` (or has some option/flag to control this). However this approach might be problematic as due the backwards compatibility, we cannot change the default behaviour of admin REST API endpoint http://localhost:8081/auth/admin/realms/test and skip returning identity-providers. Introducing another flag just for the purpose of our admin console is also not that pretty. So ATM my vote is to go with separate endpoint under https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/tree/main/rest/admin-ui-ext .

Note that pagination in "Identity providers" settings can be handled as separate follow-up tasks to this task...

### Discussion

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/21076

### Motivation

_No response_

### Details

_No response_

issue_title: Guava vulnerable to insecure use of temporary directory
issue_body:
**Source**: Trivy
**Location**: opt/keycloak/lib/lib/main/com.google.guava.guava-30.1-jre.jar
Package: com.google.guava:guava
Installed Version: 30.1-jre
Vulnerability CVE-2023-2976
Severity: MEDIUM
Fixed Version: 32.0.0
Link: [CVE-2023-2976](https://avd.aquasec.com/nvd/cve-2023-2976)
Use of Java's default temporary directory for file creation in `FileBackedOutputStream` in Google Guava versions 1.0 to 31.1 on Unix systems and Android Ice Cream Sandwich allows other users and apps on the machine with access to the default Java temporary directory to be able to access the files created by the class.

Even though the security vulnerability is fixed in version 32.0.0, we recommend using version 32.0.1 as version 32.0.0 breaks some functionality under Windows.

issue_title: Group list isn't filtered based on permission like user lists
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

account/api

### Describe the bug

Same as #19655 but with respect to the Keycloak Admin REST API
When invoking the `GET /groups` service and groups have fine-grained authorization enabled, the returned list of groups is not affected by the configured permissions.

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

`GET /groups` service returns only the groups for which the current user has permissions to query.

### Actual behavior

`org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.GroupsResource` does not implement any permission filter, while `org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.UsersResource` implements it in this method:

`private Stream<UserRepresentation> toRepresentation(RealmModel realm, UserPermissionEvaluator usersEvaluator, Boolean briefRepresentation, Stream<UserModel> userModels) {`


### How to Reproduce?

Setup:
- Create policy P1 based on realm role R1 and P2 based on role R2
- Create user U with realm role R1 and permissions of `realm-management/query-groups`
- Configure groups G1 and G2 and enable fine-grained permissions on both. Associate all the permission scopes of G1 to the policy P1 and all the permission scopes of G2 to the policy P2 and

Test execution:
- Get an access token for user U and then invoke the /groups API
- It returns both G1 and G2

Expected:
- Only G1 is returned

issue_title: Service Account Impersonation fails and results in weird browser state
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

I'd like to impersonate service account users.

Currently, while I can click on `impersonate` in the admin console e.g. the account console doesn't seem to like that and doesn't log me in.
Additionally, this somehow breaks some cookie state, as when I try to log in as another user, it says I'm already logged in to the service account.

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

Service Account impersonation works (might be a feature request) or the impersonation of service account users is disabled in the frontend so that I don't even try to do it and get confused why it doesn't work.

### Actual behavior

I can impersonate service account users but it doesn't work with the account console. Moreover, my browser is in a weird state where I can't log in normally anymore (without clearing cookies).

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a new client
    - Enable `Client Authentication`
    - Enable `Service Account roles`
2. Go to the `Service account roles` tab
3. Click on the username in the info box which says _"To manage detail and group mappings, click on the username ..."_
4. On the user info page click on the `Action` dropdown in the top right and hit `Impersonate`
5. Observe that the account console loads, but no user is logged in.
6. Hit the `Login` button to try to login as another user.
7. Enter the login details and login.
8. Observe the _"You are already logged into this session with another user '...'."_ error message.
9. Trying to go back to the application and logging in again yields the same error.

The browser state can of course be cleared by removing all cookies, or impersonating some real user and signing out there.

### Anything else?

The account API explicitly disables access to service account users:

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/7d3423ca5fe0cff17d9105ebf3b17f1ad4f1074f/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/account/AccountLoader.java#L135-L137

issue_title: During password reset, the baseURL is not shown on the info page after browser restart
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [ ] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

If the user closes their browser after requesting a reset-credential email, the redirect_uri is forgotten when the user clicks the link in the email.

NOTE: This is expected behaviour (see the discussion on this issue), but the fact that the link "Back to application" is not shown on the info page after password-reset is not expected and this should be fixed (See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/21127#issuecomment-1882737532 for the details).

### Version

18 (RH-SSO 7.6)

### Expected behavior

1. User opens the following endpoint: `{KEYCLOAK}/realms/{REALM}/protocol/openid-connect/forgot-credentials?client_id={CLIENT_ID}&response_type=code&redirect_uri={REDIRECT_URI}`
2. User enters email and submit
3. User closes browser
4. User clicks reset-password link in email
5. User enters password twice and submits
6. User is sent to `/auth/realms/{REALM}/login-actions/required-action` endpoint and shown the message `Your account have been updated`. There should be link "Back to application" with the baseUrl of the client corresponding to {CLIENT_ID}

### Actual behavior

In step 6, There is no link "Back to application" with the baseUrl of the client corresponding to {CLIENT_ID}


### How to Reproduce?

See "Actual behavior" (step 6 is the error, which is different from the expected behavior).

If the user does NOT close the browser (skips step 3), everything works as expected.

### Anything else?

I was directed to use the `/forgot-credentials` endpoint in #20867
commit_message:
During password reset, the baseURL is not shown on the info page after browser restart
closes #21127

Signed-off-by: mposolda <mposolda@gmail.com>

issue_title: User administration: It is not possible to assign a user to a subgroup of an assigned parent group in order to obtain more rights
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

I have the following group structure:

![groupStructure](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/54103655/98b70e76-0e5e-4513-9b19-e16e3703b1bb)

The following roles are now in the respective groups:
IT: it-role
![itRole](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/54103655/f91a4fb0-b598-4ca6-9974-ed3ed0f1fb38)

IT/Department: department-role
![departmentRole](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/54103655/878b500c-6085-44c7-a36c-c31d19a976d3)

IT/Department/Backend: backend-role
![backendRole](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/54103655/559b84d0-355a-4b3e-ba87-665d322c3583)

IT/Department/Frontend: frontend-role
![frontendRole](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/54103655/b255f76f-47ef-422c-b9d8-785aaeb83d45)

The principle of group hierarchy when assigning roles is as follows: If a user is in the group IT/Department. He gets the roles from the group IT and the subgroup: Department. 
So: it-role & department-role

In this example, I have created a user who I have assigned to the group: IT/Department. As described in the picture, he is in the groups IT and Department, and has the following rights from the groups.
![userGroup](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/54103655/017ff440-4e1e-4447-9bff-1d4b57e66ce4)

So far so good, now to the problem:

If I now want to give my user more rights by assigning him to the subgroup "Frontend" of IT/Department via the user administration, this is not possible because the item is greyed out.
![joinSubGroup](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/54103655/2e0bd36e-34bd-47fc-abd0-de6009f74da5)

In my opinion, in this case it should be possible to go deeper into the group structure. Because this way you understand that you cannot go deeper because it has the same properties from the subgroups, which is not true.

Furthermore, it would be clearer for the group assignment in the user administration if the general group tree structure could be displayed there. So that you can see directly which parent groups and subgroups there are.
So this view:
![groupStructure](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/54103655/98b70e76-0e5e-4513-9b19-e16e3703b1bb)

If you add the user to the subgroup via the group administration, this is possible. But it should also be possible via the user administration.
![Screenshot from 2023-06-23 14-33-48](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/54103655/514e453b-5c19-4c57-a02b-59766f27e955)


### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

That you can also assign a user to a subgroup via the user administration if the user is already in the parent group.
Furthermore, it would be helpful to have the tree structure of the groups in the user administration.

### Actual behavior

-

### How to Reproduce?

By explaining the problem, it should be possible to recreate the problem.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: GroupResource POST /children cannot update existing subgroups
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

There was an undocumented change in Admin client API for GroupResource endpoint
`POST /{realm}/groups/{id}/children`
https://www.keycloak.org/docs-api/21.1.1/rest-api/index.html#_groups_resource

in version 21.1.x caused most likely by this commit (line 159):
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/cf61a65198ba5f18ceb787c36ad8a33c34bbcdbb#diff-d233e82db465ac2b1ad22b124ab0bae3714f02d58c108c29afbbb935653d3ceaR159

Prior to this version, the behavior was "create or update a subgroup", now it throws **HTTP status error 409 Conflict** when the subgroup passed in the attribute already exists instead of updating it. This breaks existing implementations and I am not aware of any implementation replacing the previous function. I have not found anything neither in the migration guide or the API doc itself. 

Is this a bug or intentional behavior? In case of the latter, is there any recommended way how to update the subgroup?

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

Calling the API will either ignore or better - update attributes of the existing subgroup when the subgroup with the given name and/or ID already exists

### Actual behavior

API throws 409 Conflict

### How to Reproduce?

```
GroupResource resource = client.realm("...").groups().group("...");
GroupRepresentation g = resource.toRepresentation();
GroupRepresentation sg = g.getSubGroups().get(0);
Response response = resource .subGroup(sg); // this throws 409
```

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Support for JWE IDToken and UserInfo tokens in OIDC brokers
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

We have an external OpenID provider that uses encrypted tokens, in Keycloak we configured this identity provider from /.well-known/openid-configuration and everything seems to be working except that keycloak failing to recognize that token is encrypted when doing validation and straight up fails with invalid token...

I know that Keycloak itself can encrypt its own tokens, so why it fails to recognize JWE and why cannot decrypt it?

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

When other identity provider is using JWE and encrypting token, Keycloak should recognize it and decrypt it (or at least validate token as JWE), not fail to validate token because it cannot parse JWE into JWT

### Actual behavior

Keycloak is failing to construct JWSInput because input is encrypted token, altho in header there is "enc" and encryption specified

### How to Reproduce?

Add any external Identity OpenID provider that uses encrypted tokens, can be done even Keycloak to Keycloak if brokered keycloak is encrypting tokens

### Anything else?

_No response_
